description:
Offered: every semester
This course investigates the stylistic, social, persuasive, and literary dimensions of creative nonfiction. Students read and discuss contemporary nonfiction and gain experience writing in its various sub-genres, which may include memoir, biography, essays, and literary journalism. Students hone strong writing through workshops, critiques, and revision and reflect on their development as a writer all while compiling portfolios of their work in the course.
Prerequisite(s): ENGL 1200 or HON 1200
Meets General Education Requirement(s): AIA
